nodes.description: In FH-I, the CYP11B1/CYP11B2 chimeric gene causes aldosterone synthase to be regulated by ACTH rather than angiotensin II, producing glucocorticoid- suppressible aldosterone excess.

evidence.reference_title: Familial forms and molecular profile of primary hyperaldosteronism.
evidence.snippet: the synthesis of aldosterone becomes to be regulated by ACTH instead of by angiotensin II

nodes.description: Ion-channel defects in FH-II, FH-III, and FH-IV converge on adrenal glomerulosa membrane depolarization and calcium signaling, which induce aldosterone synthase and aldosterone biosynthesis.
nodes.id: Adrenal glomerulosa depolarization and calcium signaling

evidence.reference_title: CLCN2 chloride channel mutations in familial hyperaldosteronism type II.
evidence.snippet: glomerulosa cell membrane depolarization activates voltage-gated Ca2+ channels, which induces the rate-limiting enzyme for aldosterone biosynthesis, aldosterone synthase (CYP11B2)

nodes.description: Surgery is considered for lateralizing PA and may be required in severe KCNJ5-related FH-III with adrenal hyperplasia.
nodes.id: Adrenalectomy for severe or lateralizing disease

evidence.reference_title: Hypertension with or without adrenal hyperplasia due to different inherited mutations in the potassium channel KCNJ5.
evidence.snippet: requiring bilateral adrenalectomy in childhood for blood pressure control

nodes.description: Autonomous aldosterone excess suppresses renin and drives mineralocorticoid hypertension, with variable potassium wasting and hypokalemia.

evidence.reference_title: Primary Aldosteronism: An Endocrine Society Clinical Practice Guideline.
evidence.snippet: Primary aldosteronism (PA), a primary adrenal disorder leading to excessive aldosterone production by one or both adrenal glands, is a common cause of hypertension.

evidence.reference_title: CLCN2 chloride channel mutations in familial hyperaldosteronism type II.
evidence.snippet: The plasma aldosterone level in primary aldosteronism is constitutively elevated despite low levels of the normal upstream regulator renin; hypokalemia is variable.

meta.association: Causal gain-of-function variant for type IV familial hyperaldosteronism

evidence.reference_title: CACNA1H Mutations Are Associated With Different Forms of Primary Aldosteronism.
evidence.snippet: We identified four germline variations in CACNA1H which affect the electrophysiological and functional properties of the channel

nodes.description: In FH-IV, gain-of-function CACNA1H variants affect the CaV3.2 T-type calcium channel, increasing calcium influx and calcium signaling in adrenal cells.

evidence.reference_title: Recurrent gain of function mutation in calcium channel CACNA1H causes early-onset hypertension with primary aldosteronism.
evidence.snippet: producing increased intracellular Ca(2+), the signal for aldosterone production

meta.association: Causal gain-of-function variant for type II familial hyperaldosteronism

evidence.reference_title: CLCN2 chloride channel mutations in familial hyperaldosteronism type II.
evidence.snippet: Eight probands had novel heterozygous variants in CLCN2, including two de novo mutations

nodes.description: In FH-II, gain-of-function CLCN2 variants increase ClC-2 chloride-channel activity in adrenal glomerulosa cells, causing membrane depolarization and increased CYP11B2 expression.

evidence.reference_title: CLCN2 chloride channel mutations in familial hyperaldosteronism type II.
evidence.snippet: mutations cause gain of function, producing membrane depolarization and increasing CYP11B2 expression

meta.association: Causal fusion gene for type I familial hyperaldosteronism

evidence.reference_title: A chimaeric 11 beta-hydroxylase/aldosterone synthase gene causes glucocorticoid-remediable aldosteronism and human hypertension.
evidence.snippet: fusing the 5' regulatory region of 11 beta-hydroxylase to the coding sequences of aldosterone synthase

nodes.description: Low-dose glucocorticoid therapy can suppress ACTH-regulated aldosterone production in glucocorticoid-remediable aldosteronism.
nodes.id: Glucocorticoid suppression for type I disease

evidence.reference_title: A chimaeric 11 beta-hydroxylase/aldosterone synthase gene causes glucocorticoid-remediable aldosteronism and human hypertension.
evidence.snippet: under control of adrenocorticotropic hormone and suppressible by glucocorticoids

nodes.description: Hypertension is the central clinical presentation of familial hyperaldosteronism and may occur early in life, especially in severe channelopathy subtypes.

evidence.reference_title: Prevalence and characteristics of familial hyperaldosteronism: the PATOGEN study (Primary Aldosteronism in TOrino-GENetic forms).
evidence.snippet: Primary aldosteronism (PA) is the most frequent cause of secondary hypertension

evidence.reference_title: Recurrent gain of function mutation in calcium channel CACNA1H causes early-onset hypertension with primary aldosteronism.
evidence.snippet: We performed exome sequencing of 40 unrelated subjects with hypertension due to primary aldosteronism by age 10.

nodes.description: Hypokalemia is variable across familial hyperaldosteronism. It is uncommon in many FH-I cases but prominent in severe FH-III.

evidence.reference_title: Differences in the clinical and hormonal presentation of patients with familial and sporadic primary aldosteronism.
evidence.snippet: FH-I is characterized by a low prevalence of hypokalemia and FH-III by a severe aldosterone excess causing hypokalemia in more than 85% of patients.

meta.association: Causal gain-of-function variant for type III familial hyperaldosteronism

evidence.reference_title: Hypertension with or without adrenal hyperplasia due to different inherited mutations in the potassium channel KCNJ5.
evidence.snippet: These individuals had severe progressive aldosteronism and hyperplasia requiring bilateral adrenalectomy in childhood for blood pressure control.

nodes.description: In FH-III, inherited KCNJ5 variants alter the potassium-channel selectivity filter so the channel conducts sodium, depolarizing adrenal glomerulosa cells.
nodes.id: KCNJ5 selectivity filter loss and sodium conductance

evidence.reference_title: Hypertension with or without adrenal hyperplasia due to different inherited mutations in the potassium channel KCNJ5.
evidence.snippet: producing increased Na(+) conductance and membrane depolarization, the signal for aldosterone production

nodes.description: Mineralocorticoid receptor antagonists are PA-specific medical therapy for aldosterone-mediated hypertension and are relevant across FH subtypes when medical management is selected.

evidence.reference_title: Primary Aldosteronism: An Endocrine Society Clinical Practice Guideline.
evidence.snippet: We suggest the use of mineralocorticoid receptor antagonists (MRAs) over epithelial sodium-channel (ENaC) inhibitors in the medical treatment of PA.

evidence.reference_title: A double-blind, randomized study comparing the antihypertensive effect of eplerenone and spironolactone in patients with hypertension and evidence of primary aldosteronism.
evidence.snippet: The antihypertensive effect of spironolactone was significantly greater than that of eplerenone in hypertension associated with primary aldosteronism.
